state_variables.name: owner
state_variables.semantic_gap: Fabric identities are X.509 certificate-based and cannot be easily mapped, unlike Ethereum addresses are 20-byte identifiers

state_variables.name: totalSupply
state_variables.semantic_gap: Explicit GetState and PutState actions in Fabric must take the place of implicit Ethereum storage

state_variables.name: balances
state_variables.semantic_gap: Ethereum mappings use implicit hashed storage, but Fabric requires explicit composite keys like balances_address with manual GetState and PutState management

statements.semantic_gap: Fabric does not have a direct counterpart for msg.sender. Using GetClientIdentity to approximate
State writing needs to be made clear. Fabric's PutState call
Mapping writing needs to be made clear. Fabric's PutState call using the composite key balances_address
In Fabric, mapping read needs to become an explicit GetState call with a composite key
There is no direct equivalent for address(0) check in Fabric. Comparing empty strings is an approximation
Solidity's uint256 arithmetic with overflow prevention. Go needs to be large.For semantic equivalency, use Int
Solidity's uint256 arithmetic with overflow prevention. Go needs to be large.For semantic equivalency, use Int
Logs of Ethereum occurrences are permanently indexed. Fabric events are only available with a subscription
Mapping read needs to be made clear. Fabric's GetState function using the composite key balances_account

events.name: Transfer
events.semantic_gap: It is not possible to reproduce Ethereum permanent indexed event logs at the contract level in Fabric. Redesigning the application layer is necessary

portability_summary.note: PB-1 is made more difficult by SimpleToken's introduction of mapping state. PB-3 is absent since there is no ether transmission. PB-5's event delivery semantics make it non-portable
